title: keetia zanzibarica subsp. zanzibarica
intro: Keetia zanzibarica subsp. zanzibarica, also known as the Zanzibar bush or the keetia plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the Keetia zanzibarica subsp. zanzibarica plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a decoction to create teas, infusions, or tinctures.
